topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting renewable energy and reducing environmental impact.

topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various methods and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of wind energy on local ecosystems
doc3: role of policy in promoting sustainability

topic.name: Digital Marketing Strategies
topic.description: This topic encompasses various techniques and approaches used in digital marketing to enhance online presence and engagement.

doc1: utilizing social media platforms for brand awareness
email marketing campaigns to increase customer retention
doc2: search engine optimization techniques for better visibility
content marketing strategies to engage target audiences
doc3: analyzing consumer behavior through web analytics

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming and improving Medicare coverage, services, and administrative processes.

Document 1: GAO Mandates Revision Act of 2016
require the Government Accountability Office (GAO) to review reported legislation
Document 2: Medicare Home Infusion Therapy Coverage Act of 2009
provide for Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy and home infusion drugs.
Document 3: Long Term Care Quality Improvement Act of 2007
require skilled nursing facilities to separately report expenditures for wages and benefits for nursing staff.
Document 4: Fair and Speedy Treatment (FAST) of Medicare Prescription Drug Claims Act of 2007
require prompt payment of clean claims to pharmacies by prescription drug plans.
Document 5: Medicare Safe Needle Disposal Coverage Act of 2011
cover as a part D drug any devices approved for home use by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
Document 6: Medicare Payment Advisory Commission (MedPAC) Reform Act of 2009
rename the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission (MedPAC) the Medicare Payment and Access Commission.
Document 7: Ensuring Seniors Access to Local Pharmacies Act of 2014
direct any prescription drug plan that has in its network one or more pharmacies located in a health professional shortage area.

topic.name: Tax Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming tax policies in the United States, focusing on deductions, credits, and tax rates.

Document 1: denies a tax deduction for income attributable to oil companies
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 2: replaces the Hope Scholarship and Lifetime Learning tax credits
establishes programs for informing the public of the American Opportunity Tax Credit
Document 3: allows a 10% energy tax credit for green roofs
defines qualified green roof property expenditures
Document 4: establishes the Religious Freedom Peace Tax Fund
allocates amounts not for military purposes
Document 5: increases individual income tax rates for taxpayers over $1 million
provides for an adjustment in the capital gains tax
Document 6: repeals provisions requiring withholding of income taxes
addresses unjustified tax hoarding
Document 7: requires retailers to pay an excise tax on single-use carryout bags
establishes the Single-Use Carryout Bag Trust Fund

topic.name: U.S. Trade Tariff Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning the temporary suspension or modification of duties on various goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to provide for the duty-free treatment of certain recreational performance outerwear.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on standard grade ferroniobium.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on hydrazine hydrate, aqueous solution.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain sports footwear with cleats valued at $6.50/pair or more but not over $10/pair.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on Strong Base Anionic Resin-Quaternary amine styrene divinylbenzene copolymer in the chloride form.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to: (1) modify the article description for cerium sulfide pigments; and (2) extend the temporary suspension of duty on such article.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain staple fibers of viscose rayon, not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.

topic.name: Defense and Intelligence Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to national defense, military operations, and intelligence activities.

Document 1: Revises the definition of 'entitlement land' for which the Secretary of the Interior is required to make payments to local governments in lieu of taxes.
Include federal land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the Department of Defense (DOD).
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Document 4: Requires that amounts made available under the Iraq Security Forces Fund shall be provided as loans subject to full repayment to the U.S. government.
Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2006.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to the management, conservation, and use of federal lands in the United States.

Document 1: Extends through FY2023 the authority for the Forest Service and the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to enter into stewardship end result contracting projects
Authorizes the Secretaries to implement eligible wildfire prevention projects in at-risk forests
Document 2: Transfers jurisdiction over approximately 200 acres of Bureau of Land Management land from the Department of the Interior to the Department of Veterans Affairs
The cemetery's boundary is modified to include such federal land.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 to double from 10 to 20 years the period of a term for grazing permits
Permits the issuance of permits and leases for a period shorter than 20 years.
Document 4: Establish the Wildlife and Hunting Heritage Conservation Council Advisory Committee to advise the Secretaries of the Interior and of Agriculture
Abolishes the Wildlife and Hunting Heritage Conservation Council.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the state of Alaska King Cove Road and Sitkinak Island
Requires the King Cove Road conveyance to be used for construction of a state road.
Document 6: Establishes in the Treasury a Trust Fund for deposit of all amounts received by the United States as bonus bids, rents, royalties under leases for exploration
for any area of federal lands or of federal submerged lands of the outer Continental Shelf.
Document 7: Authorize the Secretary of the Interior to take into trust for the Siletz Tribe additional lands that lie within the original 1855 Siletz Coast Reservation
Prohibits gaming on lands taken into trust pursuant to this Act.

topic.name: Veterans' Health and Support
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving health care, support services, and benefits for veterans and active-duty military members.

Document 1: Extends the period for presumptive service-connected disabilities for veterans.
Compensable through the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 2: Facilitates movement of injured or disabled Armed Forces members through passenger screening.
Establishes protocols for support during screening.
Document 3: Establishes a commission to study veterans' disability claims backlog.
Directs the Secretary to report on implementation of claims backlog solutions.
Document 4: Directs mental health screenings for deployed Armed Forces members.
Ensures continuity of care between Department of Defense and Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 5: Allows veterans to contribute to tax-preferred health savings accounts.
Veterans receiving care for service-connected disabilities are not disqualified.
Document 6: Authorizes use of educational assistance for test preparatory courses.
Supports veterans in obtaining professional licensing.
Document 7: Increases funding for services for homeless veterans.
Directs annual payment rate increases based on service costs.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to immigration, including provisions for refugees, minors, and criminal aliens, as well as family reunification and employment regulations.

Document 1: SSI Extension for Elderly and Disabled Refugees Act of 2011
extends eligibility period for benefits for certain aliens
Document 2: Humane Enforcement and Legal Protections for Separated Children Act
apprehension procedures for immigration enforcement-related activities
Document 3: Development, Relief, and Education for Alien Minors Act of 2009
repeal the provision making unlawful aliens ineligible for higher education benefits
Document 4: Relocation Empowerment and Placement Assistance for Iraqi Refugees Act of 2007
waives visa and related fees relating to special immigrant status
Document 5: Filipino Veterans Family Reunification Act
exempt sons and daughters of Filipino World War II veterans from numerical limitations on immigrant visas
Document 6: Criminal Alien Removal Act of 2010
directs DHS to carry out a Criminal Alien Program to identify incarcerated criminal aliens
Document 7: 10k Run for the Border Act
increases employer civil fines for hiring unauthorized aliens

topic.name: Pharmaceutical and Food Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the safety, labeling, and postmarket surveillance of drugs and food products.

Document 1: Safer Drug Assessment Technology Advancement Act
postmarket risk identification and analysis system
Document 2: Food and Product Responsibility Act of 2008
mandatory recalls of adulterated or misbranded articles
Document 3: Gluten in Medicine Identification Act of 2012
deem to be misbranded any drug intended for human use
Document 6: Food Labeling Modernization Act of 2015
require the front of food packages to bear a nutrition label
Document 7: Food and Drug Administration Safety Act of 2007
establish the Center for Postmarket Evaluation and Research for Drugs and Biologics

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety, staffing, and operational standards within the aviation industry.

Document 1: Minimum Staffing of Air Traffic Controllers Act of 2013
ensures that a commercial airport has at least two air traffic controllers on duty
Document 2: Aviation Investment and Modernization Act of 2007
modernize the air traffic control system
Document 3: FAA Reauthorization Act of 2009
reauthorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
Document 5: require each air carrier that submits an airline service quality performance report
publish such data on the Department of Transportation's website

topic.name: Unemployment and Labor Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at managing unemployment benefits, workplace rights, and compensation structures for workers.

Document 1: Amends title III (Grants to States for Unemployment Compensation) of the Social Security Act to require state laws to deny unemployment compensation to applicants unless they undergo drug testing and test negative.
Denies unemployment compensation for five years to any applicant who tests positive for drugs three or more times.
Document 2: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before January 1, 2015.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until December 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 4: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to authorize private employers to provide compensatory time off to private employees at a rate of 1 1/2 hours per hour of employment for which overtime compensation is required.
Prohibits an employer from intimidating, threatening, or coercing an employee in order to interfere with the employee's right to request or not to request compensatory time off.

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts and designations of federal buildings and facilities in the United States.

Document 1: Arbuckle Project Maintenance Complex and District Office Conveyance Act of 2016
requires the Department of the Interior to convey to the Arbuckle Master Conservancy District
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 46-02 21st Street in Long Island City, New York, as the 'Geraldine Ferraro Post Office Building'.
Document 3: Blocking Regulatory Interference from Closing Kilns Act of 2016
prohibits the Environmental Protection Agency from requiring compliance with Clean Air Act rules
Document 4: Designates the federal building currently known as Federal Office Building 8 as the 'Thomas P. O'Neill, Jr. Federal Building.'
Document 5: Growth to Excellence Act of 2011
Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow states to measure student progress toward academic achievement standards
Document 6: Reliable Home Heating Act
Directs the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration to recognize any 30-day emergency period declared by a state governor due to a shortage of residential heating fuel
Document 7: Designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 515 9th Street in Rapid City, South Dakota, as the 'Andrew W. Bogue Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'

topic.name: Federal Customer and Small Business Support
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing customer service in federal agencies and providing support to small businesses and veterans.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to develop performance measures to determine whether federal agencies are providing high quality customer service.
Requires the head of each agency to collect information from its customers regarding the quality of its customer services.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an increase in the amount of depreciable business assets that an eligible small business can expense if such business employs certain unemployed veterans.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an accelerated five-year amortization period for goodwill and other business intangibles acquired from certain small businesses.
Document 4: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program under which the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) may make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries.
Document 5: Requires each solicitation of offers by the Department of Defense (DOD) for a covered contract to include a requirement that the offeror include with the contract bid or proposal a written statement concerning compensation paid by the offeror.
Document 6: Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make grants to small businesses to assist in the commercialization of research developed by such businesses.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from collecting debts owed to the United States by certain veterans who die as a result of a service-connected disability.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the expansion and regulation of the U.S. Postal Service's powers and services.

Document 1: Expands the enumerated powers of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to include the authority to provide nonpostal services.
Removes existing conditions that limit nonpostal services to certain services offered as of January 1, 2006.
Document 6: Expands the enumerated powers of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to include the authority to provide nonpostal services.
Directs the Postmaster General to report annually to Congress on the nonpostal services provided by the USPS.

topic.name: Political and Electoral Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming political processes, electoral systems, and representation in governance.

Document 1: Beef Checkoff Modernization Act of 2009
amends the Beef Research and Information Act
Document 2: grants to states for backup paper ballots
failure of a voting system
Document 3: Fairness and Independence in Redistricting Act of 2007
prohibits redistricting until after the next apportionment
Document 4: Veterans TRICARE Choice Act
manages health care program for veterans
Document 5: Native Hawaiian Government Reorganization Act of 2012
recognizes the right of the Native Hawaiian people to reorganize
Document 6: Empowering Mass Participation to Offset the Wealthy's Electoral Role Act
revise public financing for presidential elections
Document 7: Stand By Every Ad Act of 2014
requires identification of funding sources in political communications

topic.name: Child Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation focused on the health, safety, and welfare of children, particularly in relation to vaccinations, child care, and mental health screening.

Document 1: deem a banned mercury-containing vaccine to be adulterated
requires the Secretary to prohibit the distribution of banned mercury-containing influenza vaccines to any child under the age of three years old
Document 2: establish an Office of Child Care to serve as the principal advisor on issues regarding state-funded child care programs
demonstrate the manner in which the state will meet the specific child care needs of low-income and working families
Document 3: increases the limitation on the amount of CDBG assistance that may be used to provide public services
concerned with child care, health, drug abuse, education
Document 4: prohibits federal funds from being used to establish any universal or mandatory mental health screening program
defines a screening program under this Act as any mental health screening program in which a set of individuals is automatically screened

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Government Oversight
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at government oversight, legal reforms, and public health initiatives.

Document 1: shifts the responsibility for conducting prosecutions for violations of District of Columbia laws
local prosecutor's office
Document 2: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a comprehensive health screening, monitoring, and research program
provide a forum for ensuring that health concerns of workers and the public are considered
Document 3: authorizes the Comptroller General to conduct reviews, including onsite examinations, of any credit facility established by the Federal Reserve Board
grants the Comptroller General access to all records and property of any such credit facility
Document 4: amends the federal judicial code to authorize the chief judge of a district or court of appeals to delay, toll, or otherwise grant relief from time deadlines
grants the Director of the United States Patent and Trademark Office authority to waive statutory provisions governing the filing, processing, renewal, and maintenance of patents
Document 5: authorizes the Joint Committee on Judicial Administration to establish a voluntary separation incentive payments program
authorize the Director of the District of Columbia Public Defender Service to establish such a program
Document 6: requires that the principal office of the U.S. Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims (CAVC) be in the metropolitan area of Washington, DC
allows the President to remove judges violating that residency requirement
Document 7: directs the Secretary of the Navy to transfer the decommissioned Naval Security Group Activity
requires the Secretary of the Navy to submit reports on the progress made in connection with the remediation and transfer of the property

topic.name: Tribal Land and Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation concerning the management and designation of tribal lands, including provisions for gaming and environmental protections.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Indian Advancement Act to repeal the declaration that specified land accepted by the Secretary of the Interior for the benefit of the Lytton Rancheria in California shall be deemed to have been held in trust
Allows the Lytton Rancheria to conduct class II gaming activities on the land taken into trust.
Document 2: Holds all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to approximately 10 acres of federal land in trust for the Pascua Yaqui Tribe of Arizona
Prohibits gaming on such lands.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to specify the types of water bodies that are 'navigable waters' and therefore fall under the scope of the Act
Activities carried out by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) or the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers may not impinge upon states' power over land and water use.

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the accountability, transparency, and effectiveness of federal regulations and programs.

Document 1: requires a federal agency that publishes a final major rule in the Federal Register to publish, biannually while the rule remains in effect, a report on the benefits and costs of such rule to regulated entities
assess the impacts of the rule on such entities, include a determination about how the actual benefits and costs of the rule have varied from those anticipated when the rule was issued
Document 2: Prohibits any person, except with written permission from the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA), from using the phrases 'GI Bill' and 'Post-9/11 GI Bill'
Provides for violation enforcement through the Attorney General
Document 3: establish a Social Security Guarantee Program, to be administered by a Social Security Guarantee Board
Requires designation of a certified account manager by or on behalf of each covered individual to hold the individual's Social Security guarantee account assets for investment
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit specified electronic payment system network practices
Requires an electronic payment system network to disclose its contract terms to the merchant
Document 5: Requires any federal agency or business entity engaged in interstate commerce that uses, accesses, transmits, stores, disposes of, or collects sensitive personally identifiable information, following the discovery of a security breach, to notify
Allows a delay of notification for law enforcement and national security purposes
Document 6: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) to require any employer contribution made on behalf of the President, Vice President, or any political appointee to be paid only to the issuer of a plan through an American Health Benefit Exchange
not through the federal employees health benefit program
Document 7: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977, with respect to the allocation of surface mining reclamation funds to states and Indian tribes
Requires uncertified states or Indian tribes to use such allocated funds to fill voids and seal tunnels and entryways

topic.name: Federal Legislative Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and due process within federal agencies and the judicial system.

Document 1: Cost-Benefit and Regulatory Transparency Enhancement Act of 2013 requires federal agencies to report on cost-benefit analyses.
Requires publication of reports in the Federal Register.
Document 2: District of Columbia Court, Offender Supervision, Parole, and Public Defender Employees Equity Act of 2008 addresses employee service recognition for federal benefits.
Defines qualifying D.C. service for federal retirement calculations.
Document 3: Fair Payment of Court Fees Act of 2010 allows waiver of court fees in the interest of justice.
Amends Federal Rules of Civil Procedure to include fee waivers.
Document 4: Privacy Advocate General Act of 2013 establishes an independent office to represent privacy interests under FISA.
Authorizes the Privacy Advocate General to oppose government surveillance requests.
Document 5: Improving the Teaching and Learning of American History and Civics Act of 2009 promotes history and civics education through federal grants.
Establishes standards for U.S. history education.
Document 6: Due Process and Military Detention Amendments Act of 2013 reinforces constitutional rights for detainees in the U.S.
Prohibits military detention of individuals captured in the U.S.
Document 7: ACCOUNT Act requires federal agency approval for costly conferences and mandates transparency in reporting.
Ensures that conferences align with the core mission of the agency.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing financial, immigration, and land management issues in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the President...to designate a private, nonprofit organization as the Haitian-American Enterprise Fund
Require the Fund to end its reinvestment cycle not later than December 31, 2021
Document 2: Provides for the payment of passport fees directly to the Department of State rather than to the U.S. Treasury
Expresses the sense of Congress that new revenues directed to the Department should be used to reduce passport costs for consumers
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to sell and quitclaim a parcel of National Forest System land
Requires the Bonneau Baptist Church to pay to the Secretary cash consideration in an amount equal to the market value of such land
Document 4: Prohibits a state or local government from receiving federal financial assistance for a minimum of one year
Directs the Attorney General to report each year to Congress regarding state or local jurisdictions
Document 5: Amends the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1991 to increase from up to 12 to up to 32 the number of positions under the Architect of the Capitol
Makes certain amounts appropriated for FY2007-FY2010 to the AOC available for acquisition of real property
Document 6: Directs the Chief of the Office of Citizenship of the Department of Homeland Security to provide grants
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to provide that a legal U.S. resident who demonstrates English fluency will satisfy the residency requirement
Document 7: Establishes an independent Financial Stability Council to review, and approve or prohibit issuance of, any rule or regulation
Grants Council actions precedence in any conflict with any regulatory action of a federal financial regulator

topic.name: Environmental and Community Development Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation aimed at promoting environmental conservation, community development, and public health initiatives.

Document 1: establish a program meeting specified criteria under which DOE sets up microlabs
accelerate technology transfer from national laboratories to the marketplace
Document 2: extend funding for land conservation partnership projects
assist states, local government, and private landowners in the conservation of land and natural resources
Document 3: amends the S.A.F.E. Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008
excludes a seller of manufactured homes from the definition of loan originator
Document 4: require the Department of the Interior to convey to the Ukpeagvik Inupiat Corporation
amends ANCSA to require Interior to convey a surface estate
Document 5: authorize the Department of Justice to award grants for mental health programs
expand purposes for which grant funds may be used under existing programs
Document 6: establishes within the Environmental Protection Agency a WaterSense program
provide financial incentives for consumers to purchase and install water efficient products
Document 7: include Juab County among those Utah counties for which Bonneville Unit funds may be provided
development of systems to allow groundwater recharge and management

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Measures on Security and Immigration
topic.description: This cluster encompasses U.S. legislative actions focusing on immigration, national security, and public safety regulations.

Document 1: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to define 'criminal gang.'
Individuals found inadmissible or deportable for criminal gang membership shall be barred from asylum; withholding of removal; temporary protected status.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Power Act to authorize the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to order emergency measures to protect the reliability of the bulk-power system.
Directs FERC to require any owner, user, or operator of the domestic bulk-power system to implement measures to protect the system against specified vulnerabilities.
Document 5: Revises mandatory elements of the secure system of transportation program.
Prohibits the entry of any container that has not complied with such standards and procedures.
